name: La Puerta de Segura y Puente de Génave — circular desde Orcera
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Orcera are set within the mountainous terrain of the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park in Jaén, Spain. The region features significant elevation changes, ranging from valleys to peaks, providing challenging climbs and exhilarating descents. Cyclists will find quiet mountain roads winding through lush forests, extensive olive groves, and past natural water features like the Orcera River. This diverse landscape offers varied riding experiences across its orographic labyrinth of valleys and calares. Best road cycling routes around Orcera The most popular road cycling route is La Puerta de Segura y Puente de Génave — circular desde Orcera, a 34.2 miles (55.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 42 minutes to complete. This moderate route features an elevation gain of 2,752 feet (839 meters) and mostly well-paved surfaces. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Orcera – tour around Benatae and La Puerta de Segura, a moderate 19.9 miles (32.0 km) path. This route includes an elevation gain of 1,249 feet (381 meters) and leads through picturesque rural villages. Local road cyclists also love the Hornos de Segura – C.F. El Campillo Shelter loop from Orcera, a 37.1 miles (59.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant climbs, often completed in about 3 hours 11 minutes. Road cycling around Orcera is defined by mountainous terrain, quiet roads, and a mix of forests and olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult routes with substantial elevation gains. The routes in Orcera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Orcera's varied terrain.
tips: Small town located in the center of the Sierra de Segura on a hillside near the Amurjo river. Spectacular cycling scenery through a labyrinth of valleys, gorges and mountains.

It is a beautiful municipality on the banks of the Guadalimar river. It is located at the foot of the Sierra del Segura and is a strategic point for adventurers who want to explore it, both on foot and by bicycle.

Small picturesque town located at the entrance of the Sierra de Segura mountains. The town sits on the Guadalquivir river. It dates back to the 1st century BC, with the construction of a bridge over the Guadalquivir river, part of an important Roman road that existed in this valley and an important crossroads in trade between Levante and Andalusia.

waypoints.name: Génave Bridge over the Guadalquivir River
